title: cascarilla officinalis
intro: Cascarilla officinalis, also known as sweet orange bark or cassina, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of cascarilla officinalis include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve stress and anxiety, and support the immune system. Cascarilla officinalis is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or powder, and can also be found in capsule or tablet form, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
